name: PolarPod 3.0
desc: Science mediation platform with 3D, 2D content and an AI voicebot. Live online.
fullDesc:
Context: Science mediation platform built for Jean-Louis Étienne's PolarPod project, developed in 2024.

The platform combines several content layers to make the polar scientific mission accessible:

  • Interactive 3D scenes (Three.js) to visualise the pod and its environment
  • Editorial content and media managed via Prismic (headless CMS)
  • AI Voicebot allowing the general public to query the system in natural language

Front-end built in React.js. Available online.

name: Axel Agent
desc: Voice LLM agent inside an immersive 3D environment. Multiple display formats.
fullDesc:
Context: AI demonstrator deployed at client trade shows and generative AI discovery workshops.

The agent embodies an insurance assistant: it guides the customer through claims and accident procedures in natural language, from initial report to resolution.

A voice LLM conversational agent is embedded in a real-time 3D environment built with Unreal Engine. The solution runs on two distinct display formats:

  • Looking Glass — autostereoscopic display, glasses-free holographic rendering
  • Aximmetry GreenStudio — real-time green screen compositing for live stage presentations

The agent responds in natural language and integrates coherently into the dynamically generated 3D environment.

name: Offer To Design VR
desc: VR aircraft configurator and immersive cabin tour. Presented at the Paris Air Show.
fullDesc:
Context: VR cabin configuration experience presented at the Paris Air Show 2023.

Wearing a Meta Quest 2, visitors access a fully standalone VR aircraft interior configurator: selecting cabin layouts, materials and seat configurations, then taking an immersive tour of the configured space.

Built with Unity and a rendering pipeline optimised for Quest 2.

name: Augmented Worker
desc: Modular AR application demonstrating Connected Worker use cases in industrial settings.
fullDesc:
Context: AR demonstration app built to introduce industrial clients to the benefits of augmented reality in the field. Presented at client workshops, the Semaine de l'Immersion at Carrefour Numérique, and at CampusFab.

The application is modular and configurable: each use case can be toggled independently, allowing the demo to be tailored to the audience — field worker, production manager, or industrial decision-maker.

Use cases covered:

  • Digital Twin — visualisation of industrial equipment digital twins
  • Guided hands-free procedures — step-by-step assistance overlaid on the real environment
  • Remote Assistance — remote collaboration with real-time spatial annotation

Built with Unity and the MRTK SDK for HoloLens 2.

name: AirDraw Challenge
desc: Multimodal installation — Kinect body tracking + AI recognition of air-drawn shapes.
fullDesc:
Context: Interactive installation built for a digital agency, deployed at recruitment fairs and tech events including DevFest and Symfony Live.

The system runs a real-time body tracking pipeline via Kinect v2, coupled with a neural network shape recognition model (TensorFlow.js). Visitors draw in the air freehand; the system classifies the gesture in real time and displays the result on screen.

  • Kinect v2 skeleton tracking
  • CNN classification via TensorFlow.js
  • Node.js backend, web frontend

name: Armaldo
desc: AR on a physical scale model of a military pod. Hands-free guided procedures.
fullDesc:
Context: Demonstrator built for the Defence industry, presented at the Paris Air Show 2019 for field maintenance teams.

A technician wearing a HoloLens looks at a physical scale model of a military pod. The spatial tracking system triggers the display of hands-free guided procedures overlaid on the model in real time — step-by-step navigation with gesture-based validation.

Built with Unity and the HoloLens SDK (UWP).
